title: Photos: Nationwide protests target AI data centers
excerpt: Humans First, a conservative-led nonprofit, organized nationwide protests against AI data centers on Saturday.
content_html:
Anti-AI data center protesters rally across the US

By Lauren Edmonds

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

2026-07-18T20:31:37.435Z

Read in app

America's anti-data center movement went national on Saturday.

Protesters across the US rallied against the hulking facilities as part of a nationwide demonstration organized by Humans First, a newly founded conservative nonprofit taking on what it calls "Big AI."

The group planned more than 100 demonstrations throughout the day, from Wasilla in southcentral Alaska to the sunny shores of Naples, Florida. Many states had multiple protests scheduled, including 18 in Texas, where available land, access to an electricity grid, and potential tax breaks have turned it into a prime destination for companies building AI data centers.

New Jersey hosted two protests, including one in the small borough of Kenilworth. A crowd gathered outside the local municipal court around 10 a.m. to push back against an incoming $1.8 billion AI data center.

The local planning board approved the project last May, but it has faced growing opposition since. A petition to halt construction in April gained over 12,000 signatures. About 8,500 people live in Kenilworth, according to the latest census.

"You think this is pressure? Wait 'til there's no water pressure," one protest sign read. Another: "Build community, not data centers."

Some residents arrived with noise makers — plastic horns, whistles, a drum — while others brought colored chalk to tag the nearby sidewalks. They were undeterred as heavy rain fell later in the day. Donning ponchos and sharing umbrellas, the protesters continued to chant and march.

Grassroots efforts

Humans First is led by Amy Kremer, a MAGA darling who has been at the center of some of the most prominent conservative movements since 2009. A former Tea Party member, she cofounded Women for Trump and participated in the Republican National Committee.

She also helped organize the January 6 rally for President Donald Trump in 2021, which later devolved into a riot at the US Capitol. (Kremer neither planned nor participated in the riot.)

In an earlier interview with Business Insider, Kremer said she's now using her skills as a grassroots organizer to take on the AI industry.

"I think this is the most important fight of our lifetime," Kremer said last month. "This technology could wipe us off the face of the planet."

The nonprofit advocates an "America First" approach to AI that centers everyday residents in the conversation and empowers them to decide how the technology is developed over time.

"This technology has been built on American data with American taxpayer dollars invested into these companies with American energy and American land," Kremer said. "We have no voice in how the technology is used or how it impacts our lives, and that's not right."

On Saturday, Kremer thanked those who volunteered their time and energy to the demonstrations.

"The data center boom has been sold as inevitable. It is not. Communities have every right to ask what they are giving up and what they are getting in return. America is not for sale, and our communities are not collateral," Kremer wrote on X.

The AI divide

Tech leaders have made some grand promises about AI. They have said it will turbocharge the economy, accelerate scientific progress, cure disease, and unburden humans from mundane work.

AI, however, can only advance so far without large data centers, which provide the computing power to run and train the technology. Companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and Meta rely on data centers to keep their AI products afloat. For them, data centers are essential.

More than 1,400 AI data centers had already been built or approved for construction by the end of 2025. Many more have been proposed in 2026.

AI evangelists say America needs large data centers to support the technology, bolster national security, and gain a competitive edge against China, where researchers are nipping at the heels of the most well-heeled American labs. They also say that hyperscale data centers promote economic growth and create new jobs, particularly during construction.

Critics, however, aren't sure the rewards outweigh the risks.

Many are concerned that AI data centers cause environmental damage, raise electricity bills, drain water, and degrade the overall quality of life in the communities where they are built.

Some Americans have also criticized their local governments and developers for what they say is a lack of transparency around the approval process. Others simply don't like AI, which some prominent AI leaders have repeatedly warned could gut white-collar jobs.

In recent months, Americans opposing data centers have launched petitions, swarmed planning meetings, and taken legal action to stop construction. Some cities and towns, and even states, have paused construction, while others have banned new data centers altogether. At the federal level, Democratic Reps. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Bernie Sanders have called for a nationwide moratorium.

While the backlash against AI data centers is bipartisan, Americans don't always agree on the path to preventing them.

"We do not believe in a nationwide moratorium or even a statewide moratorium. We believe that each community should have a choice in what they put in their community," Kremer said. "And that's not happening in a lot of places. It's being done behind closed doors."

Tech companies have found favor with President Donald Trump, who has made advancing AI a central part of his agenda. The Trump administration backed the Stargate Project, a $500 billion initiative to build more data centers and accelerate federal permitting.

"I guarantee you that when Republicans are no longer controlling all the chambers, they're going to be cozying up to the Democrats because, at the end of the day, all they care about is power and control," Kremer said, referring to tech leaders.

Kremer, however, defended Trump. She says the responsibility falls on Congress.

"It's Congress's responsibility, and they need to get their act together and listen to and protect the American people," Kremer said. "President Trump's executive orders, and his most recent executive order on testing frontier AI systems, need to go further."

  • AI
  • Protest
  • More
  • Donald Trump

sentences.zh: 周六，克雷默感谢那些自愿投入时间和精力参加示威活动的人们。

blocks.html_en: "The data center boom has been sold as inevitable. It is not. Communities have every right to ask what they are giving up and what they are getting in return. America is not for sale, and our communities are not collateral," Kremer wrote on X.

sentences.en: "The data center boom has been sold as inevitable.
sentences.zh: “数据中心的繁荣被认为是不可避免的。

sentences.en: It is not.
sentences.zh: 它不是。

sentences.en: Communities have every right to ask what they are giving up and what they are getting in return.
sentences.zh: 社区完全有权询问他们正在放弃什么以及他们得到了什么回报。

sentences.en: America is not for sale, and our communities are not collateral," Kremer wrote on X.
sentences.zh: 美国是卖不出去的，我们的社区也不是抵押品，”克雷默在 X 上写道。

sentences.en: Demonstrators wave signs in Imperial, California, during Saturday's nationwide protest against AI data centers.
sentences.zh: 在周六全国范围内反对人工智能数据中心的抗议活动中，加利福尼亚州帝国市的示威者挥舞着标语。

sentences.en: SANDY HUFFAKER / AFP
sentences.zh: 桑迪·赫法克/法新社

blocks.html_en: The AI divide

sentences.en: The AI divide
sentences.zh: 人工智能鸿沟

blocks.html_en: Tech leaders have made some grand promises about AI. They have said it will turbocharge the economy, accelerate scientific progress, cure disease, and unburden humans from mundane work.

sentences.en: Tech leaders have made some grand promises about AI.
sentences.zh: 科技领导者对人工智能做出了一些宏伟的承诺。

sentences.en: They have said it will turbocharge the economy, accelerate scientific progress, cure disease, and unburden humans from mundane work.
sentences.zh: 他们表示，这将推动经济发展，加速科学进步，治愈疾病，并使人类摆脱平凡的工作负担。

blocks.html_en: AI, however, can only advance so far without large data centers, which provide the computing power to run and train the technology. Companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and Meta rely on data centers to keep their AI products afloat. For them, data centers are essential.

sentences.en: AI, however, can only advance so far without large data centers, which provide the computing power to run and train the technology.
sentences.zh: 然而，如果没有大型数据中心来提供运行和训练该技术的计算能力，人工智能只能发展到此为止。

sentences.en: Companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and Meta rely on data centers to keep their AI products afloat.
sentences.zh: Anthropic、OpenAI、Google 和 Meta 等公司依靠数据中心来维持其人工智能产品的运行。

sentences.en: For them, data centers are essential.
sentences.zh: 对于他们来说，数据中心至关重要。

blocks.html_en: More than 1,400 AI data centers had already been built or approved for construction by the end of 2025. Many more have been proposed in 2026.

sentences.en: More than 1,400 AI data centers had already been built or approved for construction by the end of 2025.
sentences.zh: 到2025年底，已建成或批准建设的人工智能数据中心超过1400个。

sentences.en: Many more have been proposed in 2026.
sentences.zh: 2026 年还有更多提案被提出。

sentences.en: David Vanek held a sign opposing a data center development in Kenilworth, New Jersey.
sentences.zh: 大卫·瓦内克（David Vanek）举着反对新泽西州凯尼尔沃思数据中心开发的牌子。

sentences.en: Paola Chapdelaine/Business Insider
sentences.zh: 保拉·查普德莱恩/商业内幕

blocks.html_en: AI evangelists say America needs large data centers to support the technology, bolster national security, and gain a competitive edge against China, where researchers are nipping at the heels of the most well-heeled American labs. They also say that hyperscale data centers promote economic growth and create new jobs, particularly during construction.

sentences.en: AI evangelists say America needs large data centers to support the technology, bolster national security, and gain a competitive edge against China, where researchers are nipping at the heels of the most well-heeled American labs.
sentences.zh: 人工智能传播者表示，美国需要大型数据中心来支持该技术、加强国家安全并获得针对中国的竞争优势，而中国的研究人员正在追赶最富有的美国实验室。

sentences.en: They also say that hyperscale data centers promote economic growth and create new jobs, particularly during construction.
sentences.zh: 他们还表示，超大规模数据中心可促进经济增长并创造新的就业机会，特别是在建设过程中。

blocks.html_en: Critics, however, aren't sure the rewards outweigh the risks.

sentences.en: Critics, however, aren't sure the rewards outweigh the risks.
sentences.zh: 然而，批评者不确定回报是否大于风险。

blocks.html_en: Many are concerned that AI data centers cause environmental damage, raise electricity bills, drain water, and degrade the overall quality of life in the communities where they are built.

sentences.en: Many are concerned that AI data centers cause environmental damage, raise electricity bills, drain water, and degrade the overall quality of life in the communities where they are built.
sentences.zh: 许多人担心人工智能数据中心会造成环境破坏，增加电费，排水，并降低其所在社区的整体生活质量。

blocks.html_en: Some Americans have also criticized their local governments and developers for what they say is a lack of transparency around the approval process. Others simply don't like AI, which some prominent AI leaders have repeatedly warned could gut white-collar jobs.

sentences.en: Some Americans have also criticized their local governments and developers for what they say is a lack of transparency around the approval process.
sentences.zh: 一些美国人还批评当地政府和开发商，称其审批过程缺乏透明度。

sentences.en: Others simply don't like AI, which some prominent AI leaders have repeatedly warned could gut white-collar jobs.
sentences.zh: 其他人根本不喜欢人工智能，一些著名的人工智能领导者一再警告说，人工智能可能会削弱白领工作岗位。

blocks.html_en: In recent months, Americans opposing data centers have launched petitions, swarmed planning meetings, and taken legal action to stop construction. Some cities and towns, and even states, have paused construction, while others have banned new data centers altogether. At the federal level, Democratic Reps. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Bernie Sanders have called for a nationwide moratorium.

sentences.en: In recent months, Americans opposing data centers have launched petitions, swarmed planning meetings, and taken legal action to stop construction.
sentences.zh: 近几个月来，反对数据中心的美国人发起请愿，召开规划会议，并采取法律行动阻止建设。

sentences.en: Some cities and towns, and even states, have paused construction, while others have banned new data centers altogether.
sentences.zh: 一些城镇甚至州已经暂停了建设，而另一些则完全禁止了新的数据中心。

sentences.en: At the federal level, Democratic Reps.
sentences.zh: 在联邦层面，民主党众议员。

sentences.en: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Bernie Sanders have called for a nationwide moratorium.
sentences.zh: 亚历山大·奥卡西奥-科尔特斯和伯尼·桑德斯呼吁在全国范围内暂停。

blocks.html_en: While the backlash against AI data centers is bipartisan, Americans don't always agree on the path to preventing them.

sentences.en: While the backlash against AI data centers is bipartisan, Americans don't always agree on the path to preventing them.
sentences.zh: 尽管两党都反对人工智能数据中心，但美国人并不总是就阻止人工智能数据中心的途径达成一致。

sentences.en: Americans have held protests, launched petitions, and taken legal action to stop the construction of AI data centers.
sentences.zh: 美国人举行抗议、发起请愿并采取法律行动来阻止人工智能数据中心的建设。

sentences.en: Paola Chapdelaine/Business Insider
sentences.zh: 保拉·查普德莱恩/商业内幕

blocks.html_en: "We do not believe in a nationwide moratorium or even a statewide moratorium. We believe that each community should have a choice in what they put in their community," Kremer said. "And that's not happening in a lot of places. It's being done behind closed doors."

sentences.en: "We do not believe in a nationwide moratorium or even a statewide moratorium.
sentences.zh: “我们不相信全国范围内的暂停，甚至全州范围内的暂停。

sentences.en: We believe that each community should have a choice in what they put in their community," Kremer said.
sentences.zh: 我们相信每个社区都应该对自己的社区内容有选择权，”克雷默说。

sentences.en: "And that's not happening in a lot of places.
sentences.zh: “而这种情况在很多地方都没有发生。

sentences.en: It's being done behind closed doors."
sentences.zh: 这是在闭门进行的。”

blocks.html_en: Tech companies have found favor with President Donald Trump, who has made advancing AI a central part of his agenda. The Trump administration backed the Stargate Project, a $500 billion initiative to build more data centers and accelerate federal permitting.

sentences.en: Tech companies have found favor with President Donald Trump, who has made advancing AI a central part of his agenda.
sentences.zh: 科技公司受到了唐纳德·特朗普总统的青睐，他已将推进人工智能作为其议程的核心部分。

sentences.en: The Trump administration backed the Stargate Project, a $500 billion initiative to build more data centers and accelerate federal permitting.
sentences.zh: 特朗普政府支持“星门计划”，这是一项耗资 5000 亿美元的计划，旨在建设更多数据中心并加快联邦审批速度。

blocks.html_en: "I guarantee you that when Republicans are no longer controlling all the chambers, they're going to be cozying up to the Democrats because, at the end of the day, all they care about is power and control," Kremer said, referring to tech leaders.

sentences.en: "I guarantee you that when Republicans are no longer controlling all the chambers, they're going to be cozying up to the Democrats because, at the end of the day, all they care about is power and control," Kremer said, referring to tech leaders.
sentences.zh: “我向你保证，当共和党人不再控制所有众议院时，他们将会向民主党人示好，因为归根结底，他们所关心的只是权力和控制，”克雷默在谈到科技领袖时说道。

blocks.html_en: Kremer, however, defended Trump. She says the responsibility falls on Congress.

sentences.en: Kremer, however, defended Trump.
sentences.zh: 然而，克雷默为特朗普辩护。

sentences.en: She says the responsibility falls on Congress.
sentences.zh: 她说，责任落在国会身上。

blocks.html_en: "It's Congress's responsibility, and they need to get their act together and listen to and protect the American people," Kremer said. "President Trump's executive orders, and his most recent executive order on testing frontier AI systems, need to go further."

sentences.en: "It's Congress's responsibility, and they need to get their act together and listen to and protect the American people," Kremer said.
sentences.zh: 克雷默说：“这是国会的责任，他们需要共同采取行动，倾听并保护美国人民的声音。”

sentences.en: "President Trump's executive orders, and his most recent executive order on testing frontier AI systems, need to go further."
sentences.zh: “特朗普总统的行政命令，以及他最近关于测试前沿人工智能系统的行政命令，需要走得更远。”

blocks.html_en: Lauren Edmonds is an award-winning reporter on the Business News team. When news isn't breaking, she covers personal finance, kitchen-table economics, and paths to financial freedom, including investing, real estate, side hustles, and small business. She also writes about guaranteed and universal basic income programs in the United States.Lauren has also covered lifestyle and entertainment, digital culture, and more. She has a master's degree from the Columbia University Graduate School of Journalism and resides in New York City.Do you have an interesting story to tell? You can reach Lauren at ledmonds@businessinsider.com or on Signal at ledmonds0.07.Popular StoriesNetflix wants to be Disney when it grows up Why Hollywood is paying this 17-year-old up to $20,000 to boost film trailers with TikTok editsHere's all the free money Trump's talked about giving Americans during his second term — and where it all standsA 17-year-old earned $72,000 after investing his e-commerce profits into stocks. Here's why he bet on the tech industry.Lawmakers float a nationwide basic income experiment that would cover the cost of a 2-bedroom apartmentNearly 30,000 Americans have received about $335 million in basic income. Here are 5 takeaways. content_html_zh:
反人工智能数据中心抗议者在美国各地集会

作者：Lauren Edmonds 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

2026-07-18T20:31:37.435Z

Read in app

周六，美国的反数据中心运动席卷全国。

美国各地的抗议者集会反对这些庞大的设施，这是人类第一组织的全国性示威活动的一部分，该组织是一家新成立的保守派非营利组织，致力于所谓的“大人工智能”。

该组织计划全天举行 100 多次示威活动，从阿拉斯加中南部的瓦西拉到佛罗里达州那不勒斯的阳光海岸。 许多州都安排了多次抗议活动，其中德克萨斯州举行了 18 场抗议活动，该州可用的土地、电网和潜在的税收减免使其成为建设人工智能数据中心的公司的主要目的地。

新泽西州举办了两场抗议活动，其中一场发生在凯尼尔沃斯小行政区。 上午10点左右，一群人聚集在当地市法院外。 对抗即将投入 18 亿美元的人工智能数据中心。

当地规划委员会去年五月批准了该项目，但此后遭到越来越多的反对。 4月份一份要求停止施工的请愿书获得了超过12,000个签名。 根据最新的人口普查，凯尼尔沃思约有 8,500 人居住。

“你以为这就是压力？ 等到没有水压了，”一块抗议标语上写着。 另一条：“建立社区，而不是数据中心。”

一些居民带着噪音制造器——塑料喇叭、口哨、鼓——来到这里，而其他人则带着彩色粉笔在附近的人行道上做标记。 尽管当天晚些时候下了大雨，但他们并没有被吓倒。 抗议者身穿斗篷、共用雨伞，继续高喊口号并游行。

基层努力

“人类优先”组织由 Amy Kremer 领导，她是 MAGA 的宠儿，自 2009 年以来一直是一些最著名的保守派运动的中心人物。 作为前茶党成员，她是特朗普妇女组织的联合创始人，并参加了共和党全国委员会。

她还帮助组织了 2021 年 1 月 6 日唐纳德·特朗普总统的集会，该集会后来演变成美国国会大厦的骚乱。 （克雷默既没有计划也没有参与骚乱。）

在早些时候接受《商业内幕》采访时，克雷默表示，她现在正在利用自己作为草根组织者的技能来挑战人工智能行业。

“我认为这是我们一生中最重要的战斗，”克雷默上个月说道。 “这项技术可能会将我们从地球上抹去。”

该非营利组织倡导“美国优先”的人工智能方法，以日常居民为中心进行对话，并让他们能够决定如何随着时间的推移开发该技术。

克雷默说：“这项技术是建立在美国数据的基础上的，美国纳税人的钱投资于这些拥有美国能源和美国土地的公司。” “对于如何使用该技术或它如何影响我们的生活，我们没有发言权，这是不对的。”

周六，克雷默感谢那些自愿投入时间和精力参加示威活动的人们。

“数据中心的繁荣被认为是不可避免的。 它不是。 社区完全有权询问他们正在放弃什么以及他们得到了什么回报。 美国是卖不出去的，我们的社区也不是抵押品，”克雷默在 X 上写道。

人工智能鸿沟

科技领导者对人工智能做出了一些宏伟的承诺。 他们表示，这将推动经济发展，加速科学进步，治愈疾病，并使人类摆脱平凡的工作负担。

然而，如果没有大型数据中心来提供运行和训练该技术的计算能力，人工智能只能发展到此为止。 Anthropic、OpenAI、Google 和 Meta 等公司依靠数据中心来维持其人工智能产品的运行。 对于他们来说，数据中心至关重要。

到2025年底，已建成或批准建设的人工智能数据中心超过1400个。 2026 年还有更多提案被提出。

人工智能传播者表示，美国需要大型数据中心来支持该技术、加强国家安全并获得针对中国的竞争优势，而中国的研究人员正在追赶最富有的美国实验室。 他们还表示，超大规模数据中心可促进经济增长并创造新的就业机会，特别是在建设过程中。

然而，批评者不确定回报是否大于风险。

许多人担心人工智能数据中心会造成环境破坏，增加电费，排水，并降低其所在社区的整体生活质量。

一些美国人还批评当地政府和开发商，称其审批过程缺乏透明度。 其他人根本不喜欢人工智能，一些著名的人工智能领导者一再警告说，人工智能可能会削弱白领工作岗位。

近几个月来，反对数据中心的美国人发起请愿，召开规划会议，并采取法律行动阻止建设。 一些城镇甚至州已经暂停了建设，而另一些则完全禁止了新的数据中心。 在联邦层面，民主党众议员。 亚历山大·奥卡西奥-科尔特斯和伯尼·桑德斯呼吁在全国范围内暂停。

尽管两党都反对人工智能数据中心，但美国人并不总是就阻止人工智能数据中心的途径达成一致。

“我们不相信全国范围内的暂停，甚至全州范围内的暂停。 我们相信每个社区都应该对自己的社区内容有选择权，”克雷默说。 “而这种情况在很多地方都没有发生。 这是在闭门进行的。”

科技公司受到了唐纳德·特朗普总统的青睐，他已将推进人工智能作为其议程的核心部分。 特朗普政府支持“星门计划”，这是一项耗资 5000 亿美元的计划，旨在建设更多数据中心并加快联邦审批速度。

“我向你保证，当共和党人不再控制所有众议院时，他们将会向民主党人示好，因为归根结底，他们所关心的只是权力和控制，”克雷默在谈到科技领袖时说道。

然而，克雷默为特朗普辩护。 她说，责任落在国会身上。

克雷默说：“这是国会的责任，他们需要共同采取行动，倾听并保护美国人民的声音。” “特朗普总统的行政命令，以及他最近关于测试前沿人工智能系统的行政命令，需要走得更远。”
